title: Possible poverty targets for Côte d'Ivoire
rate2: Continuing its own speed from 2015–25 (2x typical speed)
source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform, World Bank Macro and Poverty Outlook October 2025, Mahler et al. (2026), and Mahler et al. (2025).
visdescription: The visualization shows poverty rates Côte d'Ivoire could target by 2050.

scenes.text: In 2025, Côte d’Ivoire’s poverty rate was 16.1 percent.
At the typical speed of progress, poverty will be driven down to 6.3 percent in 2050. This is one possible 2050 target for Côte d’Ivoire.
But Côte d’Ivoire moved almost twice as fast as the typical speed in the past decade. If it continues at a speed of two, it would reach a poverty rate of 2.1 percent by 2050.

It could also choose an even more ambitious target, such as moving at four times the typical speed. This would reflect some of the fastest movers we have seen. Under such a scenario, it should target a 0.3 percent poverty rate by 2050.
